title: Garlicky Crisps Norwegian Fish Fry
description: A comforting pescatarian-friendly dish featuring red pepper paste, tadka, and crunchy garlicky crisps.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of butter in a large non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Add the cornmeal and cook, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes.
Add the cod fillets and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until golden brown and cooked through.
While the fish is cooking, mix the red pepper paste and tadka spice mix in a small bowl.
Once the fish is done, remove it from the skillet and set aside.
Add the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter to the skillet and cook until it has melted.
Add the breadcrumbs and cook, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes, or until lightly toasted.
Add the garlic crisps and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute, or until crispy.
Season the breadcrumbs mixture with salt and black pepper to taste.
Add the chopped parsley to the breadcrumbs mixture and stir to combine.
Place the cooked cod fillets on a serving plate and top with the breadcrumbs mixture.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Crush the garlicky crisps in a bowl using a fork or a rolling pin to make them crunchy.
Serve the crushed garlicky crisps as a side dish to the fish fry, if desired.
